# QuickQuill

## Private, on-device meeting notes for Mac

8 followers

## Private, on-device meeting notes for Mac

8 followers

Visit website

AI notetakers

•

Transcription

QuickQuill records your meetings and turns them into transcripts and summaries, and nothing is ever sent to the cloud. No bot joins the call, and it keeps working with Wi-Fi off, which is how the demo video was recorded. It also shows live subtitles with translation while you record, and there's push-to-talk dictation for any app. No account, no subscription. Dictation is free forever, and a single $49 purchase unlocks the full meeting workflow. Requires macOS 26 on Apple Silicon.

 Overview
 Reviews
 Alternatives
 Built with
 Team
 More

Free Options [...] Hi Product Hunt, I'm a solo developer in Japan👋 I built QuickQuill because I never got comfortable with meeting notetakers. You invite some company's bot into the call, it sits there in the participant list, everyone acts like that's normal, and the recording ends up on a server you never see. The apps say they won't look at it, and maybe they don't. I just didn't want work conversations to depend on that kind of trust. QuickQuill does everything on your Mac. It records both sides of the meeting, transcribes it, shows live subtitles with translation if you need them, and writes a summary when you stop. Nothing appears in the participant list because nothing joins the call. I recorded the demo on my M1 Mac with Wi-Fi turned off.  It requires macOS 26 on Apple Silicon. Dictation is free [...] It requires macOS 26 on Apple Silicon. Dictation is free with no time limit. The meeting features are $49 once during launch, and $79 after that. I didn't want this to be another subscription, so there isn't one. Give it a try at quickquill.app🪶

results.title: The Best Mac AI Meeting Note Takers in 2026
results.content:
Alter AI meeting note taker  
 Image credit: Alter

Alter offers an excellent privacy-first solution. It’s capable of processing all recordings and transcripts locally (transcripts are strictly 100% on-device by default), while also providing optional cloud enhancements. Alter is quick and easy to set up, automatically detects meetings, and can trigger workflow automations before and after your calls. It’s an exceptional Mac-native solution, but it is also only available for Mac. If you sometimes use other platforms, or if your colleagues do, you might want to explore an alternative. In addition, Alter is positioned as a general-purpose AI assistant for your Mac, it also has a large feature set that goes beyond taking meeting notes. View Fathom’s pricing structure here |

results.title: Top 13 Best AI Meeting Note Takers for Mac in 2025 (Free ...
results.content:
## 1. TuskNotes — The Private AI meeting Note Taker for Mac

### Best for: Privacy-first, on-device meeting notes (no bots, no cloud)

TuskNotes is a Mac-native AI meeting Note Taker built for professionals who can’t risk leaks. It’s optimized for Apple Silicon and processes summaries, transcripts, and recaps 100% locally—your audio and notes never leave your device. No recording bots join your calls; TuskNotes listens from your Mac, so meetings stay natural and discreet. [...] ### Key Features

 Apple Silicon-native speed: Fast capture and processing on M-series Macs.
 100% local, zero cloud: Summaries and transcripts stay on-device for total privacy.
 Bot-free recording: No visible meeting bot; capture from your Mac, online or in-person.
 Smart summaries & action items: Clear decisions, next steps, and highlights in seconds.
 Recurring-meeting memory : Pre-meeting recap and post-meeting context, automatically.
 Search & speaker labels: Find moments fast; see who said what at a glance.

### Pricing

 Early Access: Join the waitlist (launch pricing TBA).
 Business plans (coming soon): Admin controls, secure team folders, exportable client-ready recap

## 2. Otter.ai – AI-Powered Meeting Notes for Teams [...] Fastest Setup: Tactiq wins here. Just add the Chrome extension and it starts working right away when you join meetings in your browser. No bots to set up or accounts to configure.
 Most Private On-Device AI Meeting Note Taker: TuskNotes takes the privacy crown. Everything happens on your Mac with no cloud processing. Your audio and notes never leave your computer, making it perfect for sensitive meetings.
 Best Free Plan: Fathom offers the most generous free option. You get unlimited recording and transcription on Zoom calls without any caps on meeting length or number of meetings.

iPhone [...] Intelligence enabled device. Speech transcription works on all devices. WHY PRIVATE NOTES - AI NOTE TAKER Most AI notetakers and voice recorder apps process your voice on remote servers. That means your confidential discussions - therapy sessions, doctor visits, legal consultations, salary negotiations, business strategies - travel through the internet and sit on someone else's computer indefinitely. Private Notes - AI Note Taker is different. We built this offline note taker from the ground up to be 100% local and on-device. No compromises. No "mostly private" half-measures. Paid users who want backup can enable optional iCloud sync - encrypted by Apple, with no third-party access. Sync is off by default, and we still can't see your data. When you delete a recording, it's gone forever -

results.title: 8 Best Local AI Meeting Note Takers for Mac in 2026
results.content:
| Tool | True Local? | Offline? | Bot-Free? | Processing Model | Best For |
 ---  ---  --- |
| Mumble AI | Yes, | Yes | Yes | On-device recording, transcription, speaker labels, and summary in Local Mode; Cloud Mode available | Polished private Mac meeting notes |
| Talat | Yes | Yes | Yes | On-device meeting transcription and summaries on Mac | One-time-payment local meeting notes |
| Alter | Yes, if configured locally | Yes, for local meeting mode | Yes | Local meeting transcription and speaker ID; AI notes can use Ollama, LM Studio, BYOK, or cloud providers | Regulated-industry users who want local/private workflows |
| Meetily | Yes, if configured locally | Yes | Yes | Local transcription and Ollama-based summary | Developers and self-hosted teams | [...] Its local story is meaningful. Alter's meeting page says it supports private transcriptions that happen on your device, works across Zoom, Teams, Meet, WhatsApp, and in-person meetings, and is used by Mac users who process meetings on-device.

Alter also supports local models through Ollama and LM Studio. Its documentation frames local AI as a solution for lawyers, doctors, security researchers, and anyone working with sensitive data, and says local models keep data on the Mac and can work offline.

For meeting notes, this means Alter can support a local/private workflow when configured that way. Users who want fully local processing should choose local models in settings. Users who prefer speed, larger models, or cloud-provider flexibility can also use Alter with cloud AI providers. [...] That is a meaningful privacy posture. It may be enough for many GDPR-oriented teams. But it is not local. If your requirement is "audio never leaves the device," Jamie does not meet that bar.

Jamie is best for users who care more about EU data residency and bot-free capture than strict offline processing.

Pros

 Bot-free meeting capture
 EU/Germany-based data handling
 Audio deleted after transcription
 Good structured meeting summaries
 Works across common meeting platforms

Cons

 Not local
 Not offline
 Audio is uploaded for processing
 Higher price than some alternatives
 Not the right fit for strict on-device-only requirements

### 8. Otter Desktop: Best for Existing Otter Users Who Want No Visible Bot

True local: No

Offline: No

Bot-free: Yes
